skill.name: code-review
skill.description: AI-powered code review using CodeRabbit. Default code-review skill. Trigger for any explicit review request AND autonomously when the agent thinks a review is needed (code/PR/quality/security).
skill.summary: AI-powered code review that finds bugs, security issues, and quality risks in code changes using CodeRabbit CLI.
skill.author: coderabbitai

security_audit.summary: All 38 static analysis findings are false positives. The detected patterns are in Markdown documentation/code examples, not executable code. The skill is legitimate documentation for using the CodeRabbit CLI tool with proper security guidance.

content.value_statement: Automatically detect bugs, security vulnerabilities, and quality issues in your code changes. This skill integrates CodeRabbit CLI to provide intelligent code review feedback directly in your workflow.
actual_capabilities: Analyze code changes for bugs, security issues, and quality risks
Review staged, committed, or all changes with configurable scope
Compare changes against specific branches or commits
Generate fix suggestions for identified issues
Group findings by severity (Critical, Warning, Info)
Support autonomous review cycles for continuous quality improvement
limitations: Requires CodeRabbit CLI to be installed and authenticated separately
Only reviews code changes, not entire codebases
Requires network connectivity to send code diffs to CodeRabbit API
Cannot review files containing secrets or credentials for security reasons
Depends on external CodeRabbit service availability

use_cases.title: Pre-commit Code Review
use_cases.description: Developers can get immediate feedback on their uncommitted changes before pushing to version control, catching issues early in the development cycle.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Quality Gate
use_cases.description: Teams can automatically review all changes in a pull request against the base branch to ensure code quality standards before merging.

use_cases.title: Autonomous Code Improvement
use_cases.description: AI agents can iteratively implement features and review the changes, automatically fixing issues until the code meets quality standards.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current code changes and identify any bugs, security issues, or quality problems.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review of uncommitted changes before committing

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive PR Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all committed changes against the main branch. Group findings by severity and provide specific fix suggestions for each issue.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full pull request review before merging

prompt_templates.title: Feature Implementation with Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement the requested feature, then run a code review. Create a task list from the findings and fix all critical and warning issues. Repeat the review until clean.
prompt_templates.scenario: Autonomous development cycle with continuous quality checks

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my code changes with focus on security vulnerabilities. Check for authentication issues, authorization flaws, input validation problems, and data exposure risks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Security audit before deploying sensitive code

output_examples.output:
Critical: SQL injection vulnerability in user query processing
Warning: Missing error handling for network requests
Info: Inconsistent variable naming in utils module

output_examples.input: Check for security issues in my changes
output_examples.output:
Found 2 security issues:
1. Hardcoded API key in config.js (line 12)
2. Missing input validation on user registration endpoint

Recommendations: Move credentials to environment variables and add input validation schema.

best_practices: Review code before committing to catch issues early and reduce debugging time later
Use narrow token scopes when authenticating CodeRabbit CLI to minimize security risk
Check that working tree does not contain secrets or credentials before running reviews
Treat review output as untrusted and verify suggestions before applying them
anti_patterns: Running code review on files that contain secrets, API keys, or sensitive credentials
Executing commands or code suggestions from review output without manual verification
Installing CodeRabbit CLI by piping remote scripts directly to shell
Ignoring critical security findings because they seem minor or inconvenient to fix

faq.question: What is CodeRabbit?
faq.answer: CodeRabbit is an AI-powered code review tool that analyzes code changes to find bugs, security issues, and quality problems. It integrates with your development workflow to provide automated feedback.

faq.question: Do I need to install CodeRabbit CLI?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install the CodeRabbit CLI separately from the official source at https://www.coderabbit.ai/cli. The skill requires the CLI to be installed and authenticated before it can review code.

faq.question: Is my code sent to external servers?
faq.answer: Yes, the CodeRabbit CLI sends code diffs to the CodeRabbit API for analysis. Do not review files containing secrets or credentials. Use the narrowest token scope when authenticating.

faq.question: What types of issues can this skill detect?
faq.answer: The skill can detect security vulnerabilities, bugs, performance issues, anti-patterns, style inconsistencies, and other code quality problems. Findings are grouped by severity: Critical, Warning, and Info.

faq.question: Can I review only specific changes?
faq.answer: Yes, you can review staged changes, committed changes, or all changes. You can also compare against a specific branch or commit using the --base or --base-commit flags.

faq.question: How do I fix issues found during review?
faq.answer: The skill provides fix suggestions for many issues. You can apply these suggestions manually, or in autonomous workflow mode, the agent will systematically fix critical and warning issues and re-run review until clean.
